extraction.one_line_summary: KRON ANDERSEN Søren won the 15km technical ITT in Saint-Mammès by 12 seconds over SCHACHMANN, averaging 48km/h despite being 19th on GC.
tactical_patterns: KRON ANDERSEN rode negative split strategy, being 3sec behind ASGREEN at checkpoint but maintaining power to win decisively.
SCHACHMANN lost 7sec in second half after leading at checkpoint, revealing vulnerability over technical finishing circuits.
BILBAO gained 3sec on DE GHENT at checkpoint then faded badly, suggesting poor pacing strategy on technical courses.
ALAPHILIPPE finished 24sec down on home roads where he won Tour stage, signaling genuine form deficit not just bad luck.

rider_signals.KRON ANDERSEN Søren: positive: First professional ITT win with negative split execution, jumped from 19th to 2nd GC, 48km/h average reveals hidden TT capability
rider_signals.SCHACHMANN Maximilian: neutral: Extended GC lead to nearly 1min but couldn't convert checkpoint advantage, showing second-half technical weakness
rider_signals.ASGREEN Kasper: positive: Third place and only 13sec down confirms strong early-season TT form after Kuurne-Brussels-Kuurne solo win
rider_signals.ALAPHILIPPE Julian: negative: 24sec loss on hometown circuit where he excels suggests compromised form or training focus elsewhere
rider_signals.BILBAO Peli: positive: Fifth place and only 16sec down shows unexpected TT strength for pure climber type
rider_signals.CAMPER NAUTS Patrick: negative: Sixth at 27sec disappoints as GC contender, lost critical time to rivals in compressed classification
rider_signals.PINOT Thibaut: negative: 50sec loss even on new equipment confirms persistent ITT weakness remains exploitable vulnerability
rider_signals.NIBALI Vincenzo: negative: 53sec loss as team's GC leader suggests decline continues, worse than pure climber PINOT
